message:
feat(ftui): JSON viewer tab + analytics charts & validation modules

JSON Viewer (2noh9.4.16):
- Add DetailTab::Json variant with syntax-highlighted JSON display
- Use ftui_widgets::json_view::JsonView for token-based colorized output
- Toggle with 'J' key (CassMsg::ToggleJsonView), toggles Json↔Raw
- Style JSON keys/strings/numbers/literals/punctuation with theme colors
- Shows full conversation + messages JSON when cached, hit JSON fallback
- 6 new unit tests (170 total in ui::app::tests)
- Add shortcuts::JSON_VIEW constant

Analytics plumbing (2noh9.4.18.2 continued):
- Extract AnalyticsChartData to analytics_charts module
- Add analytics_charts::render_analytics_content + load_chart_data
- Add analytics/validate module for data quality checks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","title":"Task: Theme + style system implementation on ftui (presets, opt-outs, contrast/a11y patterns)","description":"## Goal
Implement the real ftui theme system used by all widgets:
- theme presets (dark/light/high-contrast + any existing cass presets)
- role-aware styling (user/assistant/tool/system)
- zebra stripes, gutters, highlights
- opt-out env vars (NO_COLOR, CASS_NO_COLOR, CASS_NO_ICONS, CASS_NO_GRADIENT, etc.)
- a11y patterns (when `CASS_A11Y=1`) so color is not sole carrier of meaning

## Notes
- This is the implementation counterpart of the spec work in `coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.2`.
- Prefer `ftui_style::{Theme, ThemeBuilder, StyleSheet, ColorProfile}`.

## Tests
- Unit tests for contrast computations and preset invariants.
- Snapshot tests across presets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All core panes use the same token source (no ad-hoc per-widget colors).
- Truecolor terminals get premium theme; 8/16-color terminals get safe fallback.","notes":"## Completed scope

Implemented the ftui style system and wired it into the live ftui TUI view path.

### 1) New ftui style-system module
- Added `src/ui/style_system.rs` with:
  - `UiThemePreset` (`Dark`, `Light`, `Catppuccin`, `Dracula`, `Nord`, `HighContrast`)
  - `StyleOptions` env-driven opt-outs:
    - `NO_COLOR`, `CASS_NO_COLOR`
    - `CASS_NO_ICONS`
    - `CASS_NO_GRADIENT`
    - `CASS_A11Y`
    - optional profile override via `CASS_COLOR_PROFILE`
  - Color-profile downgrade flow (`TrueColor` / `Ansi256` / `Ansi16` / `Mono`) over entire theme slot set
  - `StyleContext` + semantic `StyleSheet` tokens for app/pane/text/status/row/role/gutter styling
  - A11y role-marker fallback strategy to ensure color is not the sole signal

### 2) App wiring to use semantic style tokens
- Updated `src/ui/app.rs`:
  - Added style state in `CassApp` (`theme_preset`, `style_options`)
  - Added `resolved_style_context()` and `selected_hit()` helpers
  - Integrated style context with theme toggle and state-load flows
  - Implemented actual ftui `view()` rendering using semantic tokens (query/header, results pane, detail pane, status line)
  - Added responsive breakpoint + density-aware row rendering helpers

This moves ftui rendering off ad-hoc colors and onto a centralized token source.

### 3) Acceptance-criteria alignment
- Presets + opt-outs: implemented.
- Contrast/a11y patterning: implemented in style context + role markers.
- Truecolor vs fallback profiles: implemented via downgrade pass.
- Core pane token source: implemented in current ftui view rendering path.

## Validation
- `cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `cargo check --all-targets` ✅
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `cargo test style_system::tests --lib` ✅ (8 tests)
- `cargo test ui::app::tests --lib` ✅ (11 tests)

## Notes
- Existing workspace is multi-agent and contains unrelated in-flight changes outside this bead; this work was kept focused to `src/ui/style_system.rs` and `src/ui/app.rs` for style-system delivery.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:58.811941817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:56:13.274010365Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:42:17.883013681Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","ftui","style","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:38.553479922Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:38.962901148Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:39.370250843Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:58.811941817Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.16","title":"Task: FocusGraph navigation system on ftui (directional traversal, focus trapping, focus ring)","description":"## Why
The current TUI uses a simple FocusRegion enum (Results/Detail) with manual tracking and focus_flash_until timer. ftui provides a proper FocusGraph for navigation that handles the complexity of modals, nested widgets, and directional movement.

## What
Replace FocusRegion with ftui_widgets::focus::FocusGraph. Define focus nodes:
1. **SearchBar** (text input focus)
2. **FilterPills** (each pill focusable for edit/delete)
3. **ResultsList** (item selection focus)
4. **DetailPane** (scroll focus)
5. **CommandPalette** (modal focus trap)
6. **ExportModal** (modal focus trap)
7. **HelpOverlay** (modal focus trap)

FocusGraph handles:
- Directional navigation (Up from Results → SearchBar, Down from SearchBar → Results)
- Tab/Shift+Tab cycling through all nodes
- FocusTrap ensures modal focus doesn't leak to background widgets
- Visible focus ring indicator (colored border or glow effect) on focused widget

## Implementation
- Use frame.register_hit() for each focusable region
- FocusTrap activated when any modal is open
- Focus ring rendered as themed border highlight (uses agent accent colors)
- Replaces manual focus_region tracking and focus_flash_until timer

## Tests
- Unit: FocusGraph navigation sequence tests (Tab cycles, directional moves)
- Unit: Modal focus trapping prevents escape
- Snapshot: Focus ring renders correctly in each theme
- E2E: Full keyboard navigation flow (search → results → detail → modal → back)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All existing focus transitions preserved (no regression)
- Tab order matches logical UI flow
- Modals trap focus; Esc releases
- Focus ring visible in all 6+ themes with WCAG contrast
- CASS_DISABLE_ANIMATIONS=1 disables focus transition animations

## Depends On
- 2noh9.3.1 (layout foundation must exist first)
- 2noh9.2.3 (Program skeleton must be running)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:18.698317676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:37:26.454045275Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.16","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:29.107442839Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.16","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:18.698317676Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.16","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:26.431410925Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.17","title":"Task: Clipboard integration via OSC52 on ftui (copy paths, content, code blocks; works over SSH)","description":"## Why
The current TUI has basic clipboard via crossterm Paste events, but lacks reliable cross-terminal clipboard write support. OSC52 is the escape sequence that allows terminal apps to read/write the system clipboard without external dependencies — critical for multi-machine search (works over SSH).

## What
Leverage ftui-extras/clipboard for OSC52 clipboard integration:
1. **Copy selected result path+line** to clipboard (y or Ctrl+Y)
2. **Copy detail pane content** to clipboard (Ctrl+C in detail view without selection)
3. **Copy code block** from detail view (keybinding on specific code block)
4. **Copy search query** to clipboard
5. **Paste from clipboard** into search bar (Ctrl+V — unified API replacing raw crossterm Paste)

## Implementation
- ftui-extras clipboard module handles terminal capability detection (OSC52 support probe)
- Graceful fallback: if OSC52 not supported, try xclip/xsel/pbcopy
- Visual feedback: brief toast notification on successful copy ("Copied path to clipboard")
- Multi-select copy: Ctrl+Y copies all selected item paths separated by newlines

## Tests
- Unit: OSC52 escape sequence generation is correct
- Unit: Fallback chain works (OSC52 → xclip → noop with warning)
- Integration: Copy→paste roundtrip with ProgramSimulator
- E2E: Copy result path, verify clipboard content

## Acceptance Criteria
- Works over SSH sessions (OSC52 passthrough)
- Falls back gracefully on terminals without OSC52
- Toast confirms copy operation
- All existing y/Ctrl+Y behavior preserved

## Depends On
- 2noh9.2.3 (Program skeleton must be running)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:34.903656751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T04:26:26.427044174Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.17","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:33.479689506Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.17","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:34.903656751Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.2","title":"Task: Search bar UX on ftui (text input, history, suggestions, debounce, match modes)","description":"## Goal
Recreate and improve the search bar experience:
- search-as-you-type with debounce (still sub-60ms perceived latency)
- Up/Down history navigation + de-dup
- Ctrl key edits (clear, delete token)
- Enter forces immediate search
- Match modes (standard/prefix) and wildcard fallback indicator
- Search modes (lexical/semantic/hybrid) toggle

## Implementation Notes
- Use `ftui-widgets::input` or `textarea` for editable text.
- State transitions should be reducer-style and unit-testable.
- Search execution should be cancelable/coalesced (avoid piling requests during typing).

## Tests
- Unit tests for query editing + history behavior.
- Deterministic simulation tests using `ftui_runtime::ProgramSimulator` for:
  - typing "auth" and seeing results update after debounce
  - Up/Down history nav

## Acceptance Criteria
- Behavior matches parity matrix for all documented keybindings.
- No input lag or dropped keystrokes under heavy rendering.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:28:10.964810150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:41:06.586165597Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:41:06.586143136Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","search","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:41.039710098Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:41.442165218Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:40.617283649Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:28:10.964810150Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.3","title":"Task: Filters UI on ftui (agent/workspace/time/source + pills + breadcrumbs + pickers)","description":"## Goal
Port the full filter system:
- Agent filter picker
- Workspace filter picker
- Time filters (since/until, presets like today/week/30d)
- Source filter (local/remote/all and per-source selection)
- Clear filters and reset-state actions
- Pills UI with edit/remove + mouse interaction
- Breadcrumb bar showing current scope/modes (agent/workspace/date/ranking)

## Implementation Notes
- Prefer `ftui-widgets` list/table widgets for pickers.
- Time parsing must reuse existing `src/ui/time_parser.rs` logic (or an extracted shared parser), not re-invent.

## Tests

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.4","title":"Task: Results list on ftui (virtualized, multi-line items, stripes, role gutter, highlight, ranking)","description":"## Why
The current pane rendering manually iterates over hits with 2-line items, alternating stripe colors, rebuilding all widgets on every frame. ftui's VirtualizedList provides O(1) rendering for arbitrarily large result sets.

## What
Replace manual pane rendering with ftui_widgets::VirtualizedList:
- Fenwick-tree-based height predictor and viewport windowing
- Only renders visible items (critical for 100,000+ results)

Each result item renders as a custom widget:
1. **Agent icon/color badge** (using agent accent from theme — all 14 agents have distinct colors)
2. **Source path** (truncated with ellipsis)
3. **Line number** 
4. **Timestamp** (relative: '2h ago', 'yesterday', etc.)
5. **Preview snippet** (up to 3 lines with match highlighting)
6. **Multi-open queue indicator** (checkmark when queued via Ctrl+Enter)
7. **Source provenance badge** (e.g., [laptop] for remote sessions)

### Ranking Modes
Display adapts to current ranking mode (F12 cycles):
- RecentHeavy/DateNewest/DateOldest: show timestamp prominently
- Relevance/MatchQuality: show score prominently
- Balanced: balanced display

### Alternating Stripes
Even/odd items get subtle background tint for scanability, respecting current theme.

## Implementation
- Register hit regions per item for click selection
- Support keyboard navigation (j/k/Up/Down) through VirtualizedListState
- Viewport windowing means smooth scrolling even with 100K+ items
- Cache item heights for mixed-height items (compact vs cozy vs spacious density)

## Tests
- Unit: VirtualizedList with 100K items renders in <1ms
- Unit: Selection state preserved across re-renders
- Unit: Scroll position maintained when items change
- Snapshot: Result item renders correctly for each agent type (14 agents)
- Snapshot: Alternating stripes visible in both dark and light themes
- E2E: Search → scroll to item 50 → verify visible items are correct

## Acceptance Criteria
- Results list handles 100K+ items without lag
- All 14 agent colors render distinctly
- Match highlighting visible on search terms
- Multi-select checkmarks and queue indicators work
- Alternating stripes respect theme
- Scroll position stable during search-as-you-type

- 2noh9.3.1 (layout foundation)
- 2noh9.3.15 (theme for agent colors and stripes)

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.14","title":"Task: Animation + Visual FX infrastructure on ftui (spring physics, stagger, backdrop blur, gradients)","description":"## Why
The current TUI has basic animations (STAGGER_DELAY_MS=30ms, ITEM_FADE_MS=120ms, MAX_ANIMATED_ITEMS=15, focus flash 220ms color lerp, disableable via CASS_DISABLE_ANIMATIONS=1). ftui provides a physics-based animation system (springs, timelines, stagger, presets) plus visual effects (backdrop dim/blur, text effects, gradients, alpha compositing) that can make cass visually stunning while remaining tasteful.

## What (Merged from former Phase 4A + 4E)

### Spring Animation Migration
1. **Staggered reveal**: Replace linear delay with animation::stagger + spring config. Items spring-in from slightly offset position
2. **Focus flash**: Replace hard timer with SpringConfig (mass/stiffness/damping) for natural settle
3. **Panel resize spring**: Animate layout ratio changes when switching focus (currently instant 70/30→50/50 jump)
4. **Filter pill entrance/exit**: Spring slide animation on add/remove
5. **Modal open/close**: Scale+fade spring
6. **Search-in-progress pulse**: Glow on search bar during async search
7. **Smooth scroll interpolation**: Detail pane scrolls smoothly instead of jumping

### Visual Effects
8. **Modal backdrop dim/blur**: Background content dims to 40% opacity with subtle blur (visual_fx)
9. **Header gradient**: Subtle horizontal color gradient on title/search area (RGBA compositing)
10. **Loading text effect**: Animated gradient sweep across 'Indexing...' text
11. **Agent-colored glow**: Selected result gets subtle glow in agent's accent color
12. **Smooth fade transitions**: Cross-fade when switching between views

## Implementation
- Use ftui-core animation system: SpringConfig, Timeline, stagger, presets
- Use ftui-extras visual_fx: BackdropEffect, DimConfig, BlurConfig
- Use ftui-extras text_effects: gradient, fade, typewriter
- All effects auto-disable on slow terminals via degradation system (SimpleBorders → no effects)
- Respect CASS_DISABLE_ANIMATIONS=1 env var

## Performance Budget
- Animation frame time: <2ms per spring tick
- Visual effects rendering: <4ms per frame
- Auto-disable at degradation level >= SimpleBorders

## Tests
- Unit: Spring physics converges to target within N frames
- Unit: CASS_DISABLE_ANIMATIONS=1 disables all animation
- Snapshot: Backdrop dim renders correctly at each degradation level
- Benchmark: Animation frame time stays under 2ms budget

## Acceptance Criteria
- All existing animations preserved (stagger, focus flash)
- New animations feel natural (spring physics, not linear)
- Visual effects tasteful and non-distracting
- Zero visual regressions on terminals without truecolor
- Performance budgets met

- 2noh9.3.1 (layout foundation for resize animations)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:59:01.996084681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:39.306457428Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","performance","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:35.769791277Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:39.306356259Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:59:01.996084681Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.15","title":"Task: Charts + data visualization on ftui (heatmap, bar charts, sparklines, analytics views)","description":"## Why
cass has rich data (session counts, token usage, agent activity) but no way to visualize it in the TUI. ftui-extras provides charts (bar, line, area, scatter) and canvas (pixel-level drawing) modules that can bring data alive in the terminal — a unique differentiator for a coding agent search tool.

## What
Leverage ftui-extras/charts and canvas for terminal data visualization:

1. **Session activity heatmap**: Calendar-style heatmap showing coding session frequency per day (like GitHub contribution graph, but in the terminal). Accessible via command palette or dedicated Analytics tab.
2. **Agent usage bar chart**: Visual breakdown of sessions by agent (Claude vs Codex vs Gemini etc.) — horizontal bars with agent accent colors.
3. **Token usage sparkline in detail view**: Show token consumption over the conversation using Sparkline with gradient colors (green=low, yellow=medium, red=high). Benefits from z9fse analytics data when available.
4. **Search frequency timeline**: Mini chart in footer showing queries per minute (useful for debugging search performance).
5. **Indexing performance chart**: Throughput over time during reindexing.

## Implementation
- Use ftui-extras charts module: BarChart, LineChart, Sparkline
- Use ftui-extras canvas module: pixel-level drawing primitives for heatmap cells
- Data sources: daily_stats table (already computed by analytics pipeline), real-time counters for search/indexing
- Charts render as normal ftui widgets composable in any layout area
- Responsive: charts adapt to available space (min 40 cols for bar chart, 20 cols for sparkline)

## Tests
- Unit: Chart rendering with known data produces expected cells
- Snapshot: Heatmap golden test with mock data
- Unit: Sparkline handles edge cases (empty data, single point, overflow)
- Integration: Charts display real data from daily_stats

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least 3 visualizations implemented
- Charts readable on 80x24 terminal
- Agent colors consistent with theme system
- Degrades gracefully: text-only summary at EssentialOnly level

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.16","title":"Task: JSON viewer for raw message inspection on ftui (collapsible tree, syntax highlighting, copy)","description":"## Why
The TUI has a Raw tab in the detail pane, but it's just unformatted text. A proper JSON viewer is invaluable for debugging connector issues, understanding agent-specific message formats, and inspecting token usage data (extra_json/extra_bin fields). No other TUI search tool offers this.

## What
Add ftui_widgets::json_view::JsonView for inspecting raw JSON data of search results and messages:
1. **Raw message JSON** with syntax-highlighted keys/values/strings/numbers/booleans
2. **Collapsible nested objects and arrays** (click or Enter to expand/collapse)
3. **Copy-to-clipboard** for individual values (via OSC52 clipboard integration)
4. **Search within JSON** content (Ctrl+F in JSON view)
5. **Path display**: Shows JSON path (e.g., $.messages[0].usage.input_tokens) for selected node

## Implementation
- Use ftui_widgets::json_view::JsonView (tree-based collapsible JSON display)
- Wire as CassMsg::ToggleJsonView — toggle with J key in detail view or command palette
- Render conditionally in the detail pane area (replaces Raw tab content when active)
- Parse message extra_json/extra_bin into serde_json::Value for display
- Syntax colors: keys=blue, strings=green, numbers=yellow, booleans=cyan, null=gray

## Tests
- Unit: JSON parsing handles all serde_json::Value variants
- Snapshot: Render known JSON structure, verify layout and colors
- Unit: Collapse/expand toggle works correctly
- E2E: Navigate to result → press J → see JSON → press J → back to normal

## Acceptance Criteria
- Toggle between normal detail view and JSON view with single keypress
- All JSON types rendered with appropriate syntax colors
- Collapse/expand for nested structures
- Copy individual values to clipboard
- Works with all 14 connector message formats

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.17","title":"Task: HTML/SVG TUI screenshot export on ftui (capture current view as shareable file)","description":"## Why
Users want to capture beautiful screenshots of their cass TUI for sharing, documentation, or bug reports without external tools. This is different from export-html (which exports conversation content) — this exports the actual TUI visual state. ftui-extras/export renders Buffer snapshots as self-contained HTML or SVG.

## What
Leverage ftui-extras/export module for TUI-to-file export:
1. **Export as HTML**: Captures exactly what the user sees, preserving all colors and formatting as a self-contained HTML file
2. **Export as SVG**: High-quality vector output for documentation, presentations, READMEs
3. **Export as plain text**: Stripped ANSI for accessibility or plain-text contexts
4. **Auto-export on asciicast stop**: Generate an HTML thumbnail of the final frame

## Implementation
- Use ftui-extras export module: buffer_to_html(), buffer_to_svg(), buffer_to_text()
- Triggered via command palette action ("Export TUI screenshot") or keybinding
- Default output: ~/Downloads/cass_screenshot_<timestamp>.{html|svg|txt}
- File format selection via command palette sub-menu
- Toast notification on successful export with path

## Tests
- Unit: buffer_to_html() produces valid HTML for known buffer state
- Unit: buffer_to_svg() produces valid SVG with correct colors
- Snapshot: Export of mock TUI state matches golden reference
- E2E: Trigger export → verify file created with correct content

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 3 formats implemented (HTML, SVG, text)
- Colors preserved accurately in HTML/SVG output
- Files are self-contained (no external dependencies)
- Output filename includes timestamp to prevent overwrites
- Works from both command palette and keyboard shortcut

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18","title":"Epic: FrankenTUI analytics dashboard (tokens, cost, tools, plans, projects)","description":"## Why
Token/usage analytics is the missing “quantified self” layer for cass: we already index the entire agent work history, and we already compute token usage/cost signals in SQLite, but there is no best-in-class terminal experience for exploring it.

This epic turns cass into the world’s most functional analytics TUI for coding-agent work by leveraging FrankenTUI (ftui) strengths: deterministic rendering, composable widgets, virtualization, charts/canvas, inspector overlays, and recording/replay for reproducible bug reports.

## User Stories (What This Must Enable)
- “How many tokens did I burn today / this week / this month?” (hour/day/week/month)
- “Which projects/workspaces are costing me the most?” (top-N workspaces; trends)
- “Which agents/models are the biggest spend?” (Codex vs Claude vs Gemini; model breakdown)
- “What is my burn rate right now?” (tokens/hour live + trailing averages)
- “How tool-heavy am I?” (tool calls per 1k tokens; per-tool breakdown)
- “How much planning am I doing?” (plan messages, plan token share)
- “Where is the data real vs estimated?” (API coverage, quality/coverage heatmaps)

## Data Sources (Already In Cass)
- SQLite analytics tables:
  - message-level fact tables: token_usage (v10), message_metrics (v11)
  - rollups: token_daily_stats (v10), usage_hourly + usage_daily (v11)
  - pricing seeds: model_pricing (v10)
- Connectors already extract model/tool/token metadata into NormalizedMessage.extra; analytics ingest reads it.

## IA / Views (Initial Target Set)
1. Overview dashboard: KPI tiles + mini sparklines (today, 7d, 30d)
2. Time-series explorer: interactive chart (metric + group-by + overlays)
3. Calendar heatmap: daily tokens or sessions with color legend + drilldown
4. Breakdowns: tables with sparklines for agents/workspaces/models (sortable, virtualized)
5. Tools view: per-tool usage (calls, avg tokens/call, cost/call)
6. Plans view: plan frequency + plan token share + drift over time
7. Coverage view: where we have API tokens vs estimates, by agent/model/source
8. Drilldown: selecting any bucket opens a filtered search/results view pre-scoped to that slice

## Interaction Model
- Command palette entry: “Analytics” (and sub-commands)
- Global filters: time range, agent, workspace, source, role, metric
- Drilldown is first-class: Enter on any chart/table row navigates to details
- Mouse support where it helps (hover tooltip on charts, scroll)
- Consistent key hints + discoverability via ftui hint registry

## Performance Principles
- Prefer rollup tables for all interactive views (O(#buckets) queries).
- Avoid full message scans; allow explicit “deep analysis” mode when necessary.
- Cache query results per (filters, metric, group-by) with fast invalidation on index refresh.
- Virtualize long lists/tables; render only visible rows.

## Testing & Logging Mandate
- Deterministic rendering snapshots via ftui harness (goldens).
- PTY e2e tests: drive the analytics UI, assert stable buffers, log frame timings.
- Every view emits structured debug logs when enabled (query timing, rows read, cache hits).

## Dependencies (Not Enforced At Epic Level)
Implementation tasks under this epic will depend on:
- ftui TUI foundation (2noh9.*)
- analytics CLI/query surface (z9fse.3)
- model_name + cost support (z9fse.11, z9fse.10)
- per-tool breakdown (z9fse.6)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:10:32.201778623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:10:32.201778623Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:10:32.201778623Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.1","title":"Task: Spec analytics dashboard IA + interactions (views, filters, drilldown, keymap)","description":"## Goal
Write the definitive interaction spec for the ftui analytics dashboards so implementation is cohesive, fast, and consistent with cass power-user workflows.

This spec must be self-contained: future implementers should not need to re-read earlier plans to understand what the analytics UI is supposed to do.

## Inputs / Constraints
- Data is already stored in SQLite rollups (usage_hourly/usage_daily/token_daily_stats) and fact tables (message_metrics/token_usage).
- Prefer rollup-driven UI queries (O(#buckets)), avoid scanning message_metrics except in explicit “deep analysis” flows.
- All time semantics are UTC unless the UI explicitly supports per-user local time zones (out of scope for v1; document that we’re UTC).
- Must be navigable entirely by keyboard; mouse is optional enhancement.

## Information Architecture (Proposed)
Top-level: add an “Analytics” surface accessible via:
- Command palette: `Analytics: Dashboard`, `Analytics: Explorer`, `Analytics: Breakdowns`, `Analytics: Tools`, `Analytics: Cost`, `Analytics: Coverage`.
- Optional hotkey: `g a` (go-to analytics) or `Alt+A`.

Inside Analytics:
1. **Dashboard** (default)
   - KPI tiles: tokens today / 7d / 30d, cost today / 30d (if available), burn rate tokens/hr, tool calls per 1k tokens, plan token share.
   - Mini sparklines per KPI.
   - “Top movers” (agents/projects) with delta vs prior period.

2. **Explorer** (time-series)
   - Primary chart area (line/area/stacked).
   - Controls: metric selector, group-by selector, overlay selector.
   - Overlay options: by agent, by workspace, by source, by model.
   - Cursor crosshair + tooltip.

3. **Heatmap** (calendar)
   - Calendar grid with color scale.
   - Toggle between session count, api tokens, cost.
   - Drilldown from a day -> Explorer pre-filtered.

4. **Breakdowns** (tables)
   - Virtualized table with sortable columns.
   - Per-row sparkline (trend over range).
   - Dimensions: agent, workspace, source, model.

5. **Tools**
   - Per-tool breakdown: calls, avg tokens/call, avg cost/call, trend.
   - Ability to filter to a tool and jump to Search view for sessions that used it.

6. **Cost/Models**
   - Models list with stacked token breakdown + USD estimate.
   - Provider-level aggregation.

7. **Coverage**
   - Coverage heatmap: % API token data vs estimated by (agent x time).
   - Highlight sources/connectors with weak coverage.

## Global Filters (Persisted While In Analytics)
- Time range: presets (today, 7d, 30d, 90d, YTD, all) + custom since/until.
- Agent filter: multi-select.
- Workspace filter: multi-select with search.
- Source filter: local/remote/source_id.
- Role filter (optional for advanced).

These should be displayed in a “filter ribbon” and mirrored in a compact “filters drawer” for small terminals.

## Drilldown Semantics (Critical)
- Enter on any bucket (chart point / heatmap cell) -> opens the main Search results view pre-scoped:
  - since/until set to bucket range
  - agent/workspace/source filters inherited
  - query empty (user can type)

- Enter on any breakdown row -> same, plus an additional filter (e.g., agent=codex).

## Keymap (Draft)
- `Tab` / `Shift+Tab`: cycle analytics subviews
- `g`: open “go to” palette within analytics
- `/`: open filter/search within current view (e.g., table search)
- `Enter`: drilldown
- `Esc`: back (pop view stack)
- `?`: help overlay (view-specific key hints)

## Visual Style (ftui-first)
- Strong typography hierarchy: big KPIs, small labels, consistent number formatting.
- Color scales:
  - tokens: cool -> warm (blue/green -> yellow -> red)
  - cost: green -> amber -> red
  - coverage: red (bad) -> green (good)
- Always include legends for heatmaps/charts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A concrete, implementable spec describing:
  - views, layouts, and minimum terminal sizes
  - filters and drilldown behaviors
  - keymap + command palette entries
  - data semantics (UTC; week/month definitions)
- Explicitly calls out which UI surfaces depend on which analytics beads:
  - z9fse.12 query library
  - z9fse.6 tools breakdown
  - z9fse.10 cost estimation
  - z9fse.11 model_name

## Testing Notes
- Define snapshot test targets (which screens, which fixtures) so engineers know what to lock down.
- Define PTY e2e paths (launch -> open analytics -> switch view -> drilldown -> exit).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:50.160059329Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T20:31:38.608901310Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T20:31:38.608869390Z","close_reason":"Completed: analytics dashboard IA + interactions spec added to docs/tui_keymap_rfc.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","design","ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:50.160059329Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.3","title":"Task: Render trace + time-travel capture (record frames/events; attach to failures; inspector view)","description":"## Goal
Make the TUI *debuggable* and *testable* at kernel level by leveraging ftui's tracing and harness tooling:
- record render traces (diff stats, timings)
- optionally record compressed frame snapshots for time-travel inspection
- optionally record asciicast output for sharing

## CLI/Config
- `cass tui --trace-render <path>`: record render trace
- `cass tui --trace-events <path>`: record event stream
- optional `cass tui --trace-bundle <dir>` to write a bug-report bundle:
  - render trace
  - event macro
  - tui_state.json
  - system capabilities snapshot

## Implementation Notes
- Use `ftui_runtime::render_trace` and/or `ftui_harness::time_travel` data formats.
- Provide a built-in inspector screen that can replay a trace bundle inside cass itself.

## Tests
- In CI, on TUI test failures, automatically write a trace bundle to `test-results/`.
- Unit tests that trace writers are gated behind flags and do not affect normal perf.

- A flaky UI bug becomes reproducible from a trace bundle.
- Trace bundle contains enough context to debug without rerunning on the original machine.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:32:03.858616009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:09:07.983788589Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T21:09:07.983763112Z","close_reason":"Implemented render trace + time-travel capture: new src/ui/trace.rs module with TraceWriter (JSONL frame/event recording), FrameRecord/EventRecord types, SystemInfo capture, trace bundle writer/reader.
